title: Starter Momentum Total-Return Guard Program
objective: Run the starter-universe momentum baseline on EODHD-managed total-return inputs so the governed queue can measure whether cleaner corporate-action handling changes the evidence shape enough to justify a broader follow-up.
branch_rationale: The retired momentum branches relied on vendor-adjusted bars plus static/sample support files. This branch changes the input contract itself by using generated EODHD reference data and split-and-dividend total-return reconstruction before deciding whether momentum should spend more governed backlog depth.
positive_hypotheses: A managed vendor-backed instrument master and action ledger can remove data-quality distortions that were contaminating benchmark-relative evidence.
Reconstructed total-return inputs can improve split and walk-forward stability enough to justify a later broad-universe follow-up if the starter-universe signal shape improves.

seed_stack.label: Current starter momentum baseline
seed_stack.rationale: Control branch using the existing vendor-adjusted EODHD path.

seed_stack.label: Managed total-return starter seed
seed_stack.rationale: Same strategy family, but running on EODHD-managed reference and corporate-action inputs.

campaign_path.label: Momentum Total-Return Starter Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Execute the managed total-return starter branch and decide whether the improved data path earns broader governed follow-up.

stop_conditions.when: The total-return starter seed passes the existing validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion rules under the current policy.
The seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially if the cleaner data path still cannot produce benchmark-relative split or walk-forward evidence.
